title: pouteria pierrei
intro: Pouteria pierrei, also known as the Camiguin wild cherry or camiguin plum, is a species of tree native to the Philippines. The medicinal properties of Pouteria pierrei lie in its bark, which contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as wound healing, anti-aging, and protection against chronic diseases. Pouteria pierrei is often used in herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, to treat various ailments, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
